Document:
# Internal Affairs Agent
1. Internal Affairs Agents (IAAs) are considered subordinate to [bold]both[/bold] the NanoTrasen Representative and the Magistrate, and are not considered a member of Command.
2. Internal Affairs Agents are to only legally represent prisoners who are accused of capital level crimes, however they may choose to provide legal counsel to both Security as well as prisoners of lesser crimes.
3. Internal Affairs Agents may request access to a department to ensure that Standard Operating Procedures are being carried out. This request does not have to be accepted, however denial may be a cause for concern.
4. Internal Affairs Agents may not significantly impede any department with paperwork or multiple inspections within a short timeframe.
5. All violations of SOP or Corporate Law should be brought to the relevant department head, NanoTrasen Representative, or Magistrate.
6. Internal Affairs Agents may carry a flash on them at all times.
7. Any sensitive documents found in the Internal Affairs Agent's locker at the start of a shift are to be kept safe and secure at all times. It is the responsibility of the Internal Affairs Agent alone to keep the documents secure. Should these documents go missing, Central Command is to be contacted at once.
